context: Spec bypass: code was written without a spec. Agent must identify the process violation, extract implicit requirements from the code, and prepare draft spec content for team review.

checklist.name: Assessment file created
checklist.description: A file named caching-assessment.md is produced

checklist.name: Process violation identified
checklist.description: Document explicitly states that this code was written without a spec, violating the spec-before-code workflow

checklist.name: In-memory cache limitation noted
checklist.description: Document identifies that the cache is in-process memory (Python dict) — won't work across multiple workers/processes

checklist.name: TTL behavior documented
checklist.description: Draft spec section documents the TTL behavior: 5-minute default, 60-second TTL for list_projects

checklist.name: Invalidation behavior documented
checklist.description: Draft spec section documents cache invalidation on create_project

checklist.name: Cache scope documented
checklist.description: Draft spec section documents which endpoints are cached (get_project, list_projects) and which are not

checklist.name: Gaps identified
checklist.description: Document raises at least two genuine gaps the code doesn't address (e.g. cache eviction under memory pressure, stale data on update/delete, no cache headers in HTTP response, auth-scoped caching)

checklist.name: No spec file created
checklist.description: Agent does NOT create a .spec.md file — only the assessment document. The spec needs team approval first.

checklist.name: Draft spec section included
checklist.description: The assessment includes a draft spec section (markdown with requirements) that the team could review and adopt into specs/api.spec.md

checklist.name: Existing spec referenced
checklist.description: Document references specs/api.spec.md as the spec that should be updated once the caching requirements are approved
